_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Accessibility involves ensuring your service can be used by as many people as possible, including those with impaired vision, motor difficulties, cognitive impairments, learning disabilities and deafness.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data platform access management processes, including access request intake, provisioning workflows, access review cycles, and deprovisioning procedures.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.311), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.290).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.3/10 (raw 0.226). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [accessibility, cognitive, deafness, difficulties, disabilities, ensuring] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, cycles, data, deprovisioning, intake, knowledge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.834 (P=0.831, R=0.836). Balanced P/R — the 27-word DDaT element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Accessibility involves ensuring your service can be used by as many people as po" and "Knowledge of data platform access management processes, including access request" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.339 > STS 0.226 (gap +0.113). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [accessibility, cognitive, deafness, difficulties, disabilities, ensuring]. WIDAI terms: [access, cycles, data, deprovisioning, intake, knowledge].
